items.title: GRPO-on-Ascend Benchmark & Accuracy-Alignment Suite
items.pitch: Build a reproducible benchmark that runs GRPO/DAPO on identical models across Ascend (910B) and NVIDIA, reporting throughput, MFU, memory, and — critically — reward/loss-curve divergence. Turn the scattered numbers in verl discussion #900 into a living, automated dashboard.
items.why: Everyone porting RL to Ascend hits accuracy drift (DAPO reward MAE ~3.7–4.3% vs GRPO ~0.3–3.3%) and nobody has a clean cross-platform baseline. High value, modest compute, and a great first project that builds deep familiarity with the whole stack.
steps: Get verl running on a single Atlas 800T A2 node (8x 910B) with vLLM-Ascend
Run GRPO on Qwen2.5-0.5B/7B on GSM8K/MATH; log throughput, MFU, peak HBM
Mirror the exact run on an NVIDIA node; diff reward curves and final accuracy
Bisect divergence to operators (npu_fusion_attention, RNG, accumulation) and publish a porting/accuracy checklist
items.minimumExperiment: Run GRPO on Qwen2.5-0.5B / GSM8K for 200 steps on one Atlas 800T A2 (910B) node and one A100 node; log reward curve + peak HBM.
items.successMetric: Cross-platform reward-curve MAE quantified (<5%) with a reproducible throughput / MFU / peak-HBM table for ≥1 model.

items.title: Sleep-Mode / Colocated Hybrid Engine for RL on Ascend
items.pitch: Implement (or emulate) vLLM 'sleep mode' on Ascend so the actor and rollout engine can share NPUs, unlocking the memory-efficient colocated hybrid engine that GPU RL relies on.
items.why: Identified across the research as the single biggest RL-specific blocker on Ascend — its absence caps per-node model size and forces disaggregated setups. Solving it is a genuine systems contribution that the whole open ecosystem (verl, OpenRLHF) needs.
steps: Profile NPU memory during a colocated GRPO step; quantify what sleep mode would free
Study vLLM's CUDA sleep-mode implementation and map it to CANN/torch_npu memory APIs
Prototype KV/weight offload-reload between rollout and training phases on Ascend
Upstream to vLLM-Ascend and validate a 7B→32B colocated GRPO run on one node
items.minimumExperiment: Profile NPU memory across one colocated GRPO step on a 7B model and prototype KV/weight offload-reload between rollout and train phases.
items.successMetric: Colocated 7B GRPO runs on a single node with ≥30% lower peak HBM vs no offload, at no accuracy loss.

items.title: Asynchronous Off-Policy RL Tuned for NPU Constraints
items.pitch: Adapt an async/off-policy recipe (AReaL-style decoupling + ExGRPO experience replay) specifically to Ascend, where the missing hybrid engine makes synchronous colocated RL inefficient.
items.why: Async RL sidesteps the exact bottleneck Ascend suffers from, and AReaL already ships a stable 'ascend' branch to build on. Off-policy replay (ExGRPO) also improves sample efficiency — doubly valuable when per-chip throughput trails GPUs.
steps: Reproduce AReaL's async pipeline on Ascend using its ascend branch + SGLang rollout
Add prioritized experience replay (ExGRPO-style) keyed on rollout correctness/entropy
Measure staleness tolerance and speedup vs synchronous verl-Ascend
Characterize the accuracy/throughput Pareto frontier under fixed NPU memory
items.minimumExperiment: Reproduce the AReaL async pipeline on Ascend (ascend branch + SGLang rollout) for a 1.5–7B GRPO run.
items.successMetric: Throughput speedup vs synchronous verl-Ascend at equal final accuracy, with a documented staleness tolerance.

items.title: FP8 RL Training on Ascend
items.pitch: Bring DeepSeek-V3-style fine-grained FP8 (tile/block-wise scaling) into the RL loop on Ascend — not just pretraining — to halve memory for actor/critic and rollouts under tight HBM.
items.why: FP8 is the most direct lever against the NPU memory wall, but FP8 *RL* (with its rollout + multiple model copies) is barely explored anywhere, let alone on Ascend. Pairs naturally with prime-rl's FP8 RL direction on GPU.
steps: Confirm Ascend FP8 GEMM support and numerical behavior for forward/backward
Apply fine-grained tile/block scaling to the policy model in a GRPO run
Handle the stability landmines (entropy collapse + FP8 noise interaction)
Compare convergence and memory vs BF16 GRPO on Ascend
items.minimumExperiment: Confirm Ascend FP8 GEMM forward/backward numerics, then run one FP8-policy GRPO step on Qwen2.5-0.5B.
items.successMetric: FP8 GRPO matches BF16 reward within 1–2% at ≥30% memory reduction, stable over 200 steps.

items.title: Single-Node R1-Zero Reproduction Cookbook on Ascend
items.pitch: Produce a clean, fully-documented recipe + repo that reproduces R1-Zero-style emergent reasoning with a small model (1.5B–7B) on a single Atlas A2 node — the 'getting started' guide the Ascend RL community lacks.
items.why: R1-Zero recipes exist on Ascend but are scattered and brittle; a polished, reproducible cookbook has outsized community impact and is very achievable. Leverages the 1-shot/spurious-rewards findings to keep data and compute tiny.
steps: Pick Qwen2.5-Math-1.5B and a verifiable math reward (GSM8K/MATH)
Run GRPO via verl-Ascend or TRL-Ascend; capture the emergent long-CoT transition
Test data-efficiency (1-shot RLVR) and document every pitfall + fix
Release a one-command repo + write-up with reward curves and ablations
items.minimumExperiment: Run GRPO on Qwen2.5-Math-1.5B / GSM8K via verl-Ascend on one A2 node and capture the long-CoT transition.
items.successMetric: A one-command repo lets a fresh user reproduce the emergent-reasoning reward curve on a single node.

items.title: Entropy-Collapse Mitigation in verl-Ascend
items.pitch: Implement Clip-Cov / KL-Cov (the entropy-mechanism fixes) inside verl's Ascend path and study whether NPU numerical noise changes the entropy-collapse dynamics seen on GPUs.
items.why: Entropy collapse caps RL reasoning gains, and the interaction between Ascend's precision differences and exploration dynamics is genuinely unstudied. A focused, publishable research contribution on top of a known fix.
steps: Reproduce entropy collapse on a GRPO reasoning run on Ascend
Implement Clip-Cov/KL-Cov token-level interventions
Compare entropy/accuracy trajectories Ascend vs GPU to isolate hardware effects
Report whether NPU accumulation noise accelerates or masks collapse
items.minimumExperiment: Reproduce entropy collapse on a GRPO reasoning run on Ascend, then add Clip-Cov / KL-Cov token-level interventions.
items.successMetric: Mitigation keeps entropy above the collapsed baseline and improves final accuracy; Ascend-vs-GPU dynamics isolated.

items.title: Memory-Lean Long-Context RL via MLA + KV Compression on NPU
items.pitch: Combine Multi-head Latent Attention and chunk-wise KV compression to make long-CoT RL feasible on memory-constrained NPUs, where the KV cache dominates rollout memory.
items.why: Long reasoning chains explode the KV cache, and the 910B's weak memory bandwidth (~36% of H100) makes this acute. MLA (~93% KV reduction) + ChunkKV directly target the bottleneck that limits reasoning-RL context length on Ascend.
steps: Quantify KV-cache memory across a long-CoT GRPO rollout on Ascend
Use an MLA-based model (DeepSeek-family) and add ChunkKV-style compression to rollouts
Measure max trainable context length and throughput gains
Check reasoning accuracy is preserved vs uncompressed baselines
items.minimumExperiment: Measure KV-cache memory across a long-CoT GRPO rollout on Ascend using an MLA model + ChunkKV-style compression.
items.successMetric: Max trainable context length increased ≥2× at equal reasoning accuracy vs the uncompressed baseline.

items.title: Auto-Porting / Operator-Coverage Tool for RL Kernels on Ascend
items.pitch: Target the operator-coverage gap directly: build tooling that detects unsupported PyTorch ops in an RL training graph and either maps them to Ascend C kernels or flags fallbacks, focused on the RL-critical path (attention, sampling, logprob).
items.why: Only ~65% of PyTorch ops auto-convert and FlashAttention has no Ascend equivalent, so kernel work is the durable moat. A tool that systematizes this for RL workloads helps every team and is a strong systems/compiler contribution.
steps: Trace a verl GRPO step and enumerate ops with no/slow Ascend support
Prioritize the RL-critical kernels (fused attention, top-k/sampling, logprob gather)
Write or optimize Ascend C kernels for the top offenders; benchmark vs fallback
Package as a profiler + coverage report integrated with torch_npu
items.minimumExperiment: Trace one verl GRPO step on Ascend and emit a coverage report of unsupported/slow ops on the RL-critical path.
items.successMetric: Coverage report plus ≥1 hand-written Ascend C kernel for a top offender, benchmarked vs the fallback.
